article_title: Sony Marks Ten Years of 1000X With $649 Collexion Headphones Built From Vegan Leather and Metal

content_preview.title: Sony Marks Ten Years of 1000X With $649 Collexion Headphones Built From Vegan Leather and Metal
content_preview.summary: Sony's 1000X THE COLLEXION launches at $649.99 on the series' 10th anniversary, featuring 12-mic ANC, Edge-AI audio upscaling, and luxury materials.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Sony launched the 1000X THE COLLEXION on May 19, 2026, pricing its most expensive wireless headphones to date at $649.99 in the United States. The model is positioned as a luxury tier above the WH-1000XM6 and is timed to mark ten years since the original MDR-1000X debuted in 2016, according to [Sony Electronics](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/sony-electronics-unveils-1000x-the-collexion-where-iconic-sound-meets-refined-design-302775515.html).

## What We Know

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece with clear sections (Overview, What We Know, What We Don't Know, Analysis). Appropriate depth for the category. The 'What We Don't Know' section reflects good journalistic transparency about missing specifications.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources verified from local snapshots. source-0.html.gz (PRNewswire, status 200): Confirmed $649.99 USD / $849.99 CAD pricing, Platinum/Black colors, 10th anniversary / MDR-1000X 2016 debut, vegan leather (2-year development), 12-microphone Adaptive NC Optimizer, DSEE Ultimate Edge-AI, 360 Reality Audio Upmix (3 modes), 24h/32h battery, ~25% recycled plastic, plastic-free packaging, unidirectional carbon composite dome, Sandstone WH-1000XM6 at $459.99. source-1.html.gz (Engadget, status 200): Confirmed 8.4/10 rating, review title 'More luxurious, but far from perfect', QN3 chip shared between Collexion and XM6, moderately loud noise creeping through, reviewer prefers XM6 tuning/ANC, 40% thicker headband padding. source-2.html.gz (MacRumors, status 200): Confirmed Battery Studios/Sterling Sound/Coast Mastering mastering studios, 24h/32h battery, 5-minute quick charge for 1.5 hours, AirPods Max 2 at $549, XM6 at $449 at launch (implied: $649 minus $200), single-piece polished metal stems vs fork design, purse-style case, touch controls moved to side and rear.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims trace to cited sources. The article attributes GRAMMY award-winning mastering engineers to Sony's PR — the source says 'GRAMMY award-winning and nominated' but the article only uses 'GRAMMY award-winning', which is a minor simplification but not inaccurate. The WH-1000XM6 $449 launch price is implied by MacRumors ('$200 more than XM6 at launch') and consistent across sources. No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The Analysis section presents both the product's strengths and reviewer criticisms fairly. No sensationalism or editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate coverage. Existing Sony articles cover the Xperia 1 VIII phone and PS5 price increases — distinct topics. The WH-1000XM6 Sandstone color context is appropriately included as a simultaneous announcement.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality submission. All integrity checks pass, all sources verified, factual accuracy confirmed against three independent sources. Clean APPROVE.
